source_metadata.title_raw: Linda west City Clerk Union City 34009 Alvarado Niles Rd. Union city, CA 94587 Dear Ms. west: May 3, 1993

content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Pr actices Commission 
Linda west 
City Clerk 
Union City 
34009 Alvarado-Niles Rd. 
Union city, CA 
94587 
Dear Ms. west: 
May 3, 1993 
Re: 
Combined Campaign Reports 
Our File No. 1-93-172 
This is to confirm telephone assistance provided to you 
regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political 
Reform Act. 1 
This letter constitutes written advice under section 
84205, which states: 
The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit 
candidates and committees to file campaign statements 
combining statements and reports required to be filed by this 
title. 
union City will conduct a special election on July 27, 1993, 
to fill its vacant mayoral seat. 
Because the candidate with the 
most votes on July 27 will be elected, there will be no runoff 
election. 
The period covered by the second pre-election statement 
extends beyond the closing date of the first semi-annual statement 
for 1993. 
Therefore, candidates, their controlled committees and 
committees which are primarily formed to support or oppose 
candidates in the special election should file campaign disclosure 
statements according to the following schedule: 
1 
Government Code sections 81000-91015. 
All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulati 
section 18000, 
seq. 
All references to regulations are to 
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • 
Sacramento, CA 
95804-0807 • 
(916) 322-5660 


West, Linda 
Page 2 
No. 1-93-172 
Type of statement 
1st Pre-Election 
2nd Pre-Election 
& Semi-annual 
semi-annual 
Period Covered 
Filing Deadline 
Jan. 1 - June 12 
June 17 
June 13 - July 10 
July 15 
July 11- Dec. 31 
January 31, 1994 
If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916) 
322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
~'?rr(~'-
Lynda' Cassady 
Political Reform Consultant

sections.question_synthetic: Can candidates and committees in Union City's special election combine their campaign disclosure statements given that the second pre-election statement period extends beyond the first semi-annual statement closing date?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC authorizes candidates, their controlled committees, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose candidates in the special election to file combined campaign disclosure statements according to a specific schedule: 1st pre-election (Jan. 1 - June 12, due June 17), 2nd pre-election & semi-annual (June 13 - July 10, due July 15), and semi-annual (July 11 - Dec. 31, due January 31, 1994).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document lacks explicit QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers. The question and conclusion are reconstructed from the letter body. Advice is based on Government Code section 84205. Internal approval routing notes present at bottom of document. File number: 1-93-172.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Can candidates and committees in Union City's special election combine their campaign disclosure statements given that the second pre-election statement period extends beyond the first semi-annual statement closing date?

CONCLUSION: The FPPC authorizes candidates, their controlled committees, and committees primarily formed to support or oppose candidates in the special election to file combined campaign disclosure statements according to a specific schedule: 1st pre-election (Jan. 1 - June 12, due June 17), 2nd pre-election & semi-annual (June 13 - July 10, due July 15), and semi-annual (July 11 - Dec. 31, due January 31, 1994).
